mnemonics.title: SQL Injection Types
mnemonics.content: IBO - In-band, Blind, Out-of-band (think 'I Bring Out' the data)

mnemonics.title: Prevention Methods
mnemonics.content: PESI - Parameterized queries, Escaping (avoid), Stored procedures, Input validation + Least Privilege (as backup)

mnemonics.title: Attack Impact
mnemonics.content: CRUD - Create, Read, Update, Delete (attackers can do all database operations)

acronyms.usage: Short form used in security documentation and discussions
Security layer that filters HTTP traffic to block SQLi attacks
Framework that maps objects to database tables, often providing parameterized queries automatically
SQL command used in UNION-based SQLi to combine results from multiple tables

visualTips.visualization: Imagine user input as a locked box - parameterized queries put input in a sealed container that the database treats as pure data, never as executable code
Think of input fields as 'gates' - SQL injection slips unauthorized commands through the same gate meant for normal data
Like a yes/no game with the database - ask questions, observe if page loads normally (yes) or differently (no) to extract information bit by bit
Database accounts as employees - give each only keys to their specific room, not master keys to entire building

analogies.analogy: Like a bouncer checking ID but attacker writes their own ID card with whatever name they want - the system accepts it as valid
Like a pre-written form where you only fill in blanks - you can't change the form's structure, only provide data for the blanks
Like playing 20 questions with a silent friend who only nods or shakes head - you infer the answer from their responses
Like planting a time bomb in a package - it sits harmless at first but explodes when someone opens it later
